branchingScenario.title: AN36.1-7 | Mouth, Pharynx & Palate — Clinical Case

startScreen.startScreenSubtitle: A 62-year-old man presents with difficulty swallowing and regurgitation of undigested food. Work through this case exploring the anatomy of the tongue, pharynx, and related structures.

endScreens.endScreenSubtitle: You correctly identified the anatomical basis of Zenker's diverticulum and demonstrated strong knowledge of tongue innervation and pharyngeal anatomy.
You showed some understanding but missed key concepts. Review tongue innervation, Killian's dehiscence, and Waldeyer's ring.
Revisit cranial nerve supply to the tongue, pharyngeal constrictors, and the lymphoid ring of the pharynx.

params.text:
Case Presentation

A 62-year-old man presents with progressive dysphagia over 6 months. He reports regurgitation of undigested food several hours after eating, halitosis, and a gurgling sensation in his neck. A barium swallow reveals a posterior pharyngeal pouch — a Zenker's diverticulum.

You recall that this herniates through Killian's dehiscence, the weak area between the two parts of the inferior pharyngeal constrictor muscle. The motor supply to the pharyngeal constrictors is provided by the vagus nerve (CN X) via the pharyngeal plexus.

On examination, his tongue movements appear normal. The primary protrusor of the tongue is the genioglossus muscle.

branchingQuestion.question: During examination, you test tongue sensation. Which nerve carries general sensation from the anterior two-thirds of the tongue, and which nerve carries taste from the same region?

alternatives.text: Lingual nerve (CN V3) for general sensation; chorda tympani (CN VII) for taste
Glossopharyngeal nerve (CN IX) for both general sensation and taste

feedback.title: Not quite
feedback.subtitle: CN IX supplies both sensation and taste, but to the posterior one-third. The anterior two-thirds receives general sensation from the lingual nerve (V3) and taste from the chorda tympani (VII).

alternatives.text: Hypoglossal nerve (CN XII) for general sensation; vagus (CN X) for taste

feedback.title: Incorrect
feedback.subtitle: CN XII is purely motor (supplies all tongue muscles except palatoglossus). The lingual nerve (V3) provides general sensation and the chorda tympani (VII) carries taste from the anterior two-thirds.

params.text:
Further Investigation

Correct! The lingual nerve (V3) provides general sensation and the chorda tympani (VII) carries taste from the anterior two-thirds of the tongue.

You note the patient also has enlarged tonsils. The ring of lymphoid tissue at the pharyngeal entrance is called Waldeyer's lymphatic ring, comprising the palatine, pharyngeal (adenoid), tubal, and lingual tonsils.

The parotid duct (Stensen's duct) opens into the vestibule opposite the upper second molar tooth. On examination, saliva flow from this duct is normal.

The soft palate contains five muscles that control its movement during swallowing and speech.

branchingQuestion.question: The patient requires surgery for his Zenker's diverticulum. Which nerve supplies all intrinsic and extrinsic muscles of the tongue (with one exception), and what is that exception?

alternatives.text: Hypoglossal nerve (CN XII) supplies all tongue muscles except palatoglossus (supplied by CN X)
Glossopharyngeal nerve (CN IX) supplies all tongue muscles except genioglossus

feedback.title: Not quite
feedback.subtitle: CN IX provides sensation and taste to the posterior third of the tongue but is not motor to tongue muscles. The hypoglossal nerve (XII) supplies all tongue muscles except palatoglossus.

alternatives.text: Vagus nerve (CN X) supplies all tongue muscles except styloglossus

feedback.title: Incorrect
feedback.subtitle: CN X supplies the pharyngeal constrictors and palatoglossus. The hypoglossal nerve (CN XII) supplies all other intrinsic and extrinsic tongue muscles.

params.text:
Clinical Synthesis

Excellent! Key anatomical points from this case:

  • Tongue sensation: Anterior 2/3 — lingual nerve (V3) general, chorda tympani (VII) taste; Posterior 1/3 — glossopharyngeal (IX) both
  • Tongue motor: Hypoglossal (XII) all muscles except palatoglossus (CN X)
  • Genioglossus: Primary tongue protrusor
  • Zenker's diverticulum: Herniates through Killian's dehiscence in the inferior constrictor
  • Waldeyer's ring: Lymphoid tissue ring at pharyngeal entrance
  • Parotid duct: Opens opposite upper 2nd molar
